frameworks.name: psychophysical generalized linear model (PsyTrack-style time-varying GLM)
frameworks.one_line: trial-to-trial weights on stimuli and history covariates capture evolving decision strategy

frameworks.name: logistic regression / signal-detection style choice model
frameworks.one_line: static psychophysical weights are extended into a dynamic per-trial estimate

core_question: How can we characterize trial-to-trial changes in decision-making strategy across learning and within trained behavior?
key_assumptions: choice on each trial is driven by a weighted combination of task-relevant stimuli and task-irrelevant covariates
strategy weights vary smoothly across trials
history terms (previous choice, reward, stimulus) contribute alongside current stimulus
key_findings: method recovers trial-to-trial sensitivity to task-relevant stimuli
method also recovers weights on task-irrelevant covariates (choice, reward, stimulus history)
learning trajectories show suppression of sub-optimal strategies and adaptation to changing task statistics
shared behavioral dynamics emerge across subjects within an experimental population
interpretations: standard static behavioral analyses miss the dynamic structure of decision strategy
a flexible time-varying weight model exposes how strategies evolve and converge across subjects
